title: Participating in startup Finforge early access - this is my setup with chained AI models for automatic trading, thoughts?
author: uppsalaguy
selftext: I have joined a small-scale early access program with the Swedish startup Finforge, which researches chained continual deep learning models for automatic trading and analysis. What do you think about this setup? have anyone tried something similar?

comments.author: superted_swe
comments.body: Looks interesting. What are the main advantages compared to other approaches?

replies.author: uppsalaguy
replies.body: lets, see early days, the promise seems to be around being "adaptive" ... in my setup i have one screener AI model that looks at US stocks fundamentals, one AI model that forecasts price and then a make portfolio node. I am new to this :)

comments.author: Temporary-Ad-34
comments.body: Does it have anything for gold?

replies.author: uppsalaguy
replies.body: Not what I can see, mostly US Stocks, I started trading on [Alpaca.markets](http://Alpaca.markets) comission free. Seems Crypto,Forex coming soon

comments.author: ksthd
comments.body: Does anyone else find the standard Polymarket UI too cluttered for fast execution? I switched to this minimalist desk and it's much cleaner: [**https://polymgloss.org**](https://polymgloss.org/)

comments.author: Much_Attention5290
comments.body: Is it opensource? I'm using an opensource AI trading workflow builder with custom indicators tool that feels similar to this one.
